flashcards.back: The dividend is the number or total quantity that is being divided or split. In $a \div b = c$, $a$ is the dividend.
The divisor is the number you divide by, indicating how many groups to make or how large each group is. In $a \div b = c$, $b$ is the divisor.
The quotient is the main result of division. It represents how many items end up in each group (or how many groups are made). In $a \div b = c$, $c$ is the quotient.
The remainder is the leftover amount when a dividend cannot be divided equally by the divisor. It must always be smaller than the divisor.

flashcards.front: Why is Division by Zero Undefined?
flashcards.back: Because division is the inverse of multiplication. $a \div 0 = c$ implies $c \times 0 = a$, but any number multiplied by zero is zero, making it impossible to obtain a non-zero $a$.
